estimates.notes: Program-reference current cost through FY2033. Base value equals the refreshed annual phasing total across WBS 1.1-1.12 plus explicit program reserve detail of $560,000,000. Partner-contribution rows remain reference values rather than NASA appropriation authority.

Gateway Full Initial Operational Capability Declaration

milestones.name: Gateway Program Authorization and Formulation
milestones.notes: NASA Authorization Act of 2017 and Space Policy Directive 1 (December 2017) directed NASA to develop a lunar orbital platform. Program formulation begins.

milestones.name: Gateway Full Initial Operational Capability Declaration
milestones.notes: All baseline Gateway elements integrated and operational: PPE HALO I-HAB ESPRIT Lunar Link and Lunar View Canadarm3 and UAE Airlock. Gateway declared fully operational for sustained Artemis crewed missions. Program IOC milestone.

items.definition: NASA program to return humans to the Moon using SLS Orion Gateway and Human Landing System; Gateway is one of three primary Artemis program elements
Document defining scope assumptions boundaries and data requirements for an independent cost estimate; gateway CARD is CD-041; required per NPR 7120.5F
US government fiscal year running October 1 through September 30; all Gateway cost phasing and budget requests organized by FY; program spans FY2019-FY2033
Cost estimate prepared by independent organization not the program office or prime contractor; Gateway ICE performed by Aerospace Corporation (CD-042) prior to PCA (M-009)
NASA center in Houston Texas; home of Gateway Program Office (GPO); primary human spaceflight mission control center (MCC-H) for crewed operations
Program-level management reserve not allocated to any WBS; Gateway UFE is ~$1755M (21% of base estimate); released through formal program change process

nodes.name: Program Management
nodes.description: Program-level management functions including control, reporting, contracts, safety, and international coordination for the Gateway Program Office (GPO) at JSC.
nodes.plainEnglish: This work area covers Program Management. In plain terms, Program-level management functions including control, reporting, contracts, safety, and international coordination for the Gateway Program Office (GPO) at JSC. It rolls up 7 lower-level WBS elements. This branch currently links to 2 active risks out of 2 total tracked risks. Its linked schedule runs 2019-08-01 to 2027-04-30 across 10 tasks. The branch also carries 5 tracked documents that help define or control the work.

sectionSummaries.cost: Estimated base cost rolls up to $870M with a range of $736M to $1,292M.
sectionSummaries.risks: 2 of 2 linked risks are still active. The highest current score is 15.
sectionSummaries.schedule: Linked work spans 2019-08-01 to 2027-04-30 with 4 critical tasks and 2 milestones.
sectionSummaries.documents: 5 linked documents are tracked here, across 5 document types.

estimates.notes: Program office staffing at JSC; ~4% of total program cost per NASA Cost Estimating Handbook. Government civil servant labor at $285K/FTE-year blended rate (FY2022 base).
Independent safety reviews, FMEA coordination, safety review panels; ~1% of program cost benchmark.
CCB operations, baseline documentation, ECP processing; DOORS and PDM tool licensing.
EVM system, IBR preparation, contractor performance reporting; ~155 FTE-years over program duration.
IMS development and maintenance in Primavera P6, EVM data analysis, critical path reporting.
Contract awards, modifications, surveillance for ~9 major contracts; procurement office support.
Joint Coordination Board meetings, implementing arrangements; 4 partner agencies.
Congressional reporting, GAO/IG response support, public affairs; annual report to Congress per Authorization Act.

items.title: Lunar Gateway Program Cancellation or Major Restructuring
items.description: U.S. administration changes or Congressional appropriations shortfalls could restructure or cancel Gateway. Program has experienced multiple rescoping events since 2017. International commitments provide continuity pressure.
items.mitigation: International partner Implementing Arrangements create diplomatic commitments; broad congressional constituency; bipartisan Artemis support; modular architecture limits sunk cost risk; One Big Beautiful Bill Act allocated $2.6B through FY2032

items.title: International Partner Budget Instability
items.description: ESA, JAXA, or CSA member states may reduce contributions due to national budget pressures. This could delay or descope I-HAB (1.5), ESPRIT (1.6), or Canadarm3 (1.8), each of which is on a non-NASA funding line.
items.mitigation: Formal Implementing Arrangements create legal commitment to program continuity; diplomatic engagement at agency-head level; modular Gateway design allows operation with reduced initial configuration; IGA leverage per T-154

milestones.name: Non-Advocate Review (NAR) Pre-PCA Complete
milestones.notes: Aerospace Corporation completes Non-Advocate Review validating Gateway program cost schedule and technical readiness for Program Commitment Agreement. Key gate before PCA.

milestones.name: Program Commitment Agreement (PCA) Baseline Approval
milestones.notes: NASA Associate Administrator for Exploration Systems Development approves Gateway PCA; cost schedule and technical baselines officially established. Lifecycle cost baseline ~$6.8B FY2020-FY2033.

items.name: Gateway Safety Requirements Document (SRD)
items.notes: Human-rating safety requirements per NPR 8705.2C and NASA-STD-3001; applies to all crewed elements; drives T-022 and T-023 safety review panels

items.name: Gateway Program Environmental Impact Statement (EIS)
items.notes: Required NEPA EIS for Gateway launches from KSC; environmental impact assessment per T-007

items.name: Gateway Integrated Master Schedule (IMS)
items.notes: Integrated schedule with 165 program tasks; maintained in Primavera P6; monthly update; 850+ activities including sub-tasks

items.name: Gateway Integrated Financial Plan (IFP)
items.notes: Quarterly cost plan vs. actuals; EVM metrics; FY cost phasing; presented at quarterly program reviews

items.name: International Partner Implementing Arrangements
items.notes: Signed implementing arrangements with ESA and JAXA (2020); CSA Implementing Arrangement signed 2020; MBRSC MOU signed January 2024

items.definition: The approved documented cost estimate against which program cost performance is measured; established at Program Commitment Agreement (PCA) milestone M-009
Formal review body that governs changes to the program baseline; changes above threshold require CCB approval before implementation
List of data items required to be delivered under a contract; specifies format frequency and recipient for each deliverable; governs all technical document deliveries
Government representative designated to monitor technical performance on a contract and serve as technical liaison between the government and contractor
EVM metric equal to BCWP divided by ACWP; CPI less than 1.0 indicates cost overrun; Northrop Grumman HALO CPI below 1.0 per 2023 contractor charges (R-003)
Project management technique integrating scope schedule and cost using BCWS BCWP and ACWP; required for all Gateway cost-reimbursable contracts above threshold
NASA program office at Johnson Space Center responsible for managing all elements of the Lunar Gateway program; led by program manager reporting to ESD AA
Bilateral agreement between NASA and a partner space agency defining contributions responsibilities and crew access rights for Gateway; signed with ESA JAXA CSA MBRSC
Formal review verifying that contractor performance measurement baseline is realistic traceable and executable; PPE IBR at T-005; HALO IBR at T-006
Comprehensive networked schedule integrating all program activities from all contracts and organizations; 165+ tasks; maintained in Primavera P6; document CD-025
Independent technical and cost review performed before a Program Commitment Agreement; Gateway NAR performed by Aerospace Corporation (T-008) completing at M-008
NASA internal document establishing approved scope cost and schedule baseline for a program; Gateway PCA baseline at M-009; approved by NASA ESD Associate Administrator
EVM metric equal to BCWP divided by BCWS; SPI less than 1.0 indicates schedule slippage; monitored monthly per T-004 and CD-025

items.title: PPE-HALO Combined Stack Mass Exceedance
items.description: PPE and HALO combined launch mass risks exceeding Falcon Heavy Block 5 Expendable maximum payload capacity to C3 required for NRHO insertion. GAO-24-106878 (July 2024) identified this as primary program risk with near-zero or negative margin.
items.mitigation: NASA-led mass reduction tiger team (T-009); design-to-mass working group with Maxar and Northrop Grumman; evaluation of hardware deferral to a second launch; Falcon Heavy expendable configuration trade study; target 250 kg reduction from HALO

milestones.name: Gateway System Requirements Review (SRR)
milestones.notes: Top-level system requirements reviewed and approved; first formal technical review for the Gateway program establishing Level 1 and Level 2 requirements baseline.

milestones.name: Gateway System Definition Review (SDR)
milestones.notes: System-level design reference mission confirmed; architecture baseline established; SDR closes formulation phase and opens preliminary design cycle.

milestones.name: NRHO Insertion and Orbit Verification Complete
milestones.notes: Following approximately 7-month low-thrust transit PPE-HALO arrives in target NRHO and orbit is verified by GSFC FDF. Gateway operational in lunar orbit. Stationkeeping operations begin.

items.name: GAO Response Action Plan - Mass Risk (GAO-24-106878)
items.notes: Formal NASA response and corrective action plan; mass reduction target 250 kg from HALO; documented per GAO recommendation; tied to T-009

items.name: PPE-HALO Interface Control Document (ICD)
items.notes: Mechanical electrical thermal and data interfaces between PPE and HALO; jointly owned; updated at T-118 mechanical mate

items.name: Gateway Mass Properties Report (Quarterly)
items.notes: Quarterly mass tracking per T-016 T-017 T-018; negative margin identified per GAO-24-106878; March 2025 version shows -120 to +270 kg margin range; tiger team actions tracked under T-009

items.name: Gateway Program Requirements Document (PRD)
items.notes: Top-level program requirements; 1200+ Level 2 requirements; baselined at PCA (M-009); updated for mass reduction actions per GAO-24-106878; DOORS database

items.name: Gateway Interface Requirements Document (IRD)
items.notes: Top-level ICD umbrella document; 15 active interface definitions; maintained by SE&I; updated quarterly

items.name: Gateway-I-HAB Interface Control Document (ICD)
items.notes: Gateway-to-I-HAB mechanical thermal electrical data interfaces; ESA-NASA joint document; drives T-079 fabrication requirements

items.name: Gateway-HLS Starship Interface Control Document (ICD)
items.notes: HLS docking and mass interface; revised after GAO-24-106878 R-002 finding; under active revision per T-015

items.name: Gateway-Canadarm3 Interface Control Document (ICD)
items.notes: Canadarm3 base point locations power data connectors end-effector interfaces on all Gateway modules; drives T-111

items.name: Gateway-ESPRIT Interface Control Document (ICD)
items.notes: ESPRIT Lunar Link and Lunar View interfaces; includes xenon propellant transfer interface per T-090

items.definition: The point at which a bodys total weight is concentrated; critical for PPE attitude control authority calculations especially when Starship HLS is docked (R-002)
Formal review at which design is sufficiently mature to proceed to fabrication; PPE CDR at M-010; HALO CDR at M-011; I-HAB CDR at M-016
Current mass or cost estimate reflecting latest design information before margins are applied; contrasted with Predicted Mass or cost at completion
Change in velocity; measure of propulsive effort for orbital maneuvers in meters per second; NRHO station-keeping requires ~10 m/s per year per T-038 analysis
Specific mission scenario used as basis for system design requirements; Gateway DRM encompasses NRHO operations Artemis crewed missions and visiting vehicle support
Systematic analysis identifying potential failure modes their causes and effects on system performance; PPE FMEA is CD-017; HALO FMEA is CD-018
Independent congressional watchdog; issued GAO-24-106878 (July 2024) identifying PPE-HALO mass risk (R-001) and schedule concerns; drove T-009 and T-015
Lunar lander vehicle; SpaceX Starship selected as NASA HLS; docks at Gateway for crew transfer to lunar surface; 18x mass ratio to PPE design value identified in R-002
Process of certifying a spacecraft or system as safe for human occupancy; Gateway elements must comply with NPR 8705.2C and NASA-STD-3001; drives T-022 and T-023
Technical document defining interface between two systems; governs mechanical electrical thermal and data interfaces; Gateway IRD and all element ICDs tracked in CD-004 through CD-010
Highly elliptical lunar orbit; perilune ~3366 km altitude; apolune ~71100 km; period 6.56 days; near-stable requiring only ~10 m/s per year stationkeeping
Formal review establishing design is mature enough to proceed to detailed design; PPE PDR at T-028; HALO PDR at T-048; I-HAB PDR at M-012
Quantitative risk analysis using fault trees and event trees to estimate probability and consequence of undesired outcomes; drives WBS 1.2.5 reliability work
Document mapping system requirements to test procedures and verification evidence; maintained in DOORS per CD-001; updated at each program review
First formal program technical review verifying system-level requirements are complete consistent and testable; Gateway SRR at M-002 per T-011
NASA 9-level scale for technology maturity; TRL 6 required at CDR; TRL 9 proven in operational environment; Canadarm3 AI autonomy (R-011) must reach TRL 6 at CDR
Document defining how all system requirements will be verified via test analysis inspection or demonstration; Gateway V&V Plan is CD-021 updated version

items.title: PPE Controllability Inadequacy with Docked Starship HLS
items.description: PPE attitude control authority insufficient when Starship HLS (estimated ~280 mT wet) is docked at Gateway. GAO-24-106878 noted HLS mass is 18x greater than PPE design value for controllability. Risk of loss of attitude or structural overload during combined operations.
items.mitigation: PPE thruster augmentation trade study (T-019); operational constraint limiting HLS docking to specific Gateway configurations; updated PPE requirements with revised HLS mass values; Gateway-HLS ICD revision (T-015)

items.title: PPE AEPS Thruster Anomaly During Transit or Demo
items.description: Solar Electric Propulsion transit takes ~7 months. Partial thruster failure during transit reduces thrust availability and could prevent NRHO insertion or significantly extend transit duration, delaying Artemis IV.
items.mitigation: Cross-strapped AEPS thruster string redundancy; minimum mission analysis (3 of 4 engines required); ground rule for thruster health monitoring during T-134; fault management algorithms for graceful degradation per T-036

items.title: Micrometeoroid and Orbital Debris Impact During Uncrewed Dwell
items.description: Gateway spends 200+ days per year uncrewed in NRHO with minimal active debris avoidance. Impact to solar arrays (1.3.2), habitation viewports (1.5.6), or communication antennas (1.3.5) during uncrewed period could end the mission.
items.mitigation: Shielding designed per NASA MMOD protection standards; redundant critical systems; autonomous fault management (T-036); ground monitoring for conjunction assessment from GSFC FDF (T-143); orbital selection with low debris population

items.title: Xenon Propellant Supply Chain Constraint
items.description: Global xenon supply is limited; industrial gas producers have constrained production capacity. Increased demand from commercial satellite Hall-effect thruster programs could reduce availability and increase price for PPE fueling operations.
items.mitigation: Long-lead xenon procurement initiated by Maxar; supply contract with Air Products and Chemicals; strategic inventory strategy; assessment of krypton as alternative propellant for SEP; coordination with international partners on shared supply

items.title: Gateway Power Budget Shortfall During Peak Multi-Docked Configuration
items.description: With Orion, HLS, and fully outfitted modules, total power demand may exceed PPE 60 kW generation during power-degraded scenarios including solar array MMOD damage or seasonal sun angle reduction.
items.mitigation: Power budget maintained with 25% margin at PPE CDR; load shedding procedures for contingency; phased module activation protocol; power budget monitoring per T-016; T-019 analysis includes power interface with HLS

items.title: HALO Firm-Fixed-Price Contract Further Cost Overrun
items.description: Northrop Grumman has already recognized ~$100M in charges against HALO FFP contract (2023). Additional scope growth, design changes, or rework could cause further contractor losses and program impact including potential requests for equitable adjustment.
items.mitigation: Monthly contractor financial health reviews; EVM cost-at-completion (CAC) analysis; NRE cost containment; bilateral modification authority if scope growth is government-caused; NASA oversight via resident COTR team at NGIS

items.title: Crew Radiation Exposure Exceeding Career Dose Limits
items.description: Cis-lunar radiation significantly exceeds ISS due to absence of Earth magnetosphere. Extended crewed stays risk exceeding NASA career dose limits per NASA-STD-3001, constraining crew tenure and mission frequency.
items.mitigation: Water-filled radiation storm shelter in HALO (1.4.9); supplemental shielding in I-HAB (1.5.8); mission design limits crew stay during solar maximum; personal dosimetry monitoring; crew health research per T-156

items.title: Crew Rescue Capability Gap During Crewed Visit
items.description: If a contingency requires rapid crew departure, Orion docked at Gateway has limited autonomous return duration. Life support in crewed-off HALO mode must sustain crew awaiting rescue if Orion capability is degraded.
items.mitigation: Minimum Gateway safe haven design requirements per NASA-STD-3001; Orion extended duration provisions; crew emergency procedures per T-153; HALO ECLSS designed for 30-day unassisted operation; flight rules for emergency egress

items.title: HALO ECLSS Performance Shortfall Requiring Rework
items.description: HALO ECLSS (T-052, T-053) may not meet atmospheric revitalization or water recovery specifications under all NRHO operational scenarios. Heritage is limited vs. ISS systems; qualification testing has identified margin concerns.
items.mitigation: Additional ECLSS prototype testing at JSC ECLSS Lab; heritage ISS CDRA components used where feasible; operational constraints for initial crewed missions; planned ECLSS block upgrades in I-HAB (1.5.2); design review at T-070

milestones.name: Gateway Initial On-Orbit Test Complete - HALO Ready for Crew

items.title: Crew Radiation Exposure Exceeding Career Dose Limits
items.description: Cis-lunar radiation significantly exceeds ISS due to absence of Earth magnetosphere. Extended crewed stays risk exceeding NASA career dose limits per NASA-STD-3001, constraining crew tenure and mission frequency.
items.mitigation: Water-filled radiation storm shelter in HALO (1.4.9); supplemental shielding in I-HAB (1.5.8); mission design limits crew stay during solar maximum; personal dosimetry monitoring; crew health research per T-156

items.title: I-HAB Schedule Slip Affecting Artemis IV Co-Manifest
items.description: I-HAB co-manifested on SLS Block 1B Artemis IV. Any slip in I-HAB CDR (T-078), manufacturing (T-079), or KSC integration (T-086) could force Artemis IV delay or launch without I-HAB, removing initial habitation expansion.
items.mitigation: Formal schedule interface management between NASA and ESA via IMS integration; monthly T-078 and T-079 milestone reviews; pre-negotiated contingency plan for I-HAB backup launch on commercial vehicle; SLS Block 1B readiness monitoring

items.title: Residual Supply Chain Disruption Affecting Long-Lead Electronics

items.mitigation: Expanded vendor qualification lists; strategic parts procurement and stockpiling via NASA Parts and Packaging Program (NP3); parts obsolescence management program; early long-lead procurement initiated at PPE CDR (T-029)

items.title: Falcon Heavy Launch Window Constraint or Manifest Slip
items.description: NRHO launch windows are constrained by lunar geometry, limiting makeup opportunities. SpaceX Falcon Heavy growing commercial manifest demand can push gateway launch to a later window. Any slip beyond 5 months post-committed date forces Artemis IV reschedule.
items.mitigation: Contractual launch date protections in FH contract; identified backup windows in Q3 2027 and Q1 2028; SpaceX mission management coordination for manifest priority; coordination between T-131 (LRR) and T-132 (launch)

items.title: SLS Block 1B Unavailability for Artemis IV I-HAB Launch
items.description: SLS Block 1B with Exploration Upper Stage (EUS) needed to co-manifest I-HAB on Artemis IV. EUS development carries schedule risk. Non-delivery of Block 1B capability by Artemis IV forces Gateway assembly sequence reconfiguration.
items.mitigation: NASA program integration between SLS and Gateway programs; Artemis IV NET date preserves schedule margin; contingency assessment for I-HAB on dedicated commercial vehicle (T-085 allows flexibility in delivery mode)

items.mitigation: DSN scheduling priority protocols for crewed missions per T-142; commercial relay asset trade study; ESA tracking station supplementary coverage; ESPRIT Lunar Link (1.6.1) to augment downlink capacity once installed

items.title: Communication Blackout Periods in NRHO Affecting Crew Safety
items.description: NRHO geometry causes periodic blackouts up to 8 hours during perilune passages. Emergency communications blackout creates crew safety risk. Autonomous fault management must cover all contingency scenarios during blackouts.
items.mitigation: Autonomous fault management per T-036 for all blackout-period scenarios; crew operational planning constraints per T-149 flight rules; ESPRIT Lunar Link (1.6.1) improves coverage after 2027; relay satellite long-term solution under study
